evidence: The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.
Monetization happens through compounding relationship, not transaction. Requires 6+ year patience before peak value.
Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.
The company that 'owns' a university's career pipeline owns 10 years of talent flow from that institution.
Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.
AI didn't improve talent assessment; it may have made it worse. Information asymmetry is increasing, not decreasing.
Ivor's new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out after joining (week after next) to facilitate Handshake conversation.
Ivor suggested Handshake unprompted; V confirmed it's the 'actual perfect acquirer' with existing product-market fit with career centers and distribution to all colleges.

source_display: Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state. The gap between stated job requirements and actual selection preferences is a structural feature of hiring markets, not a bug.

source_display: Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: The 'silent scan' becomes more than a feature—it's a philosophical stance about how hiring actually works vs. how it's supposed to work.
An Indian recruiter placing US engineers said: 'I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.'
Logan's insight: Use Careerspan's high response rates as the proxy metric for convincing recruiters that 'people pay attention to our recommendations.'
If Careerspan's candidates respond at higher rates than other sources, that's sufficient proof of value. Everything else just ladders up to this single metric.

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters.
Superposition wouldn't bring users. They might not even bring significant revenue. But their logo creates a trust cascade. The brand IS the product.
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users' was: 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' This inverts the network effects playbook.
Instead of 'we're valuable because we're big,' it's 'we're valuable because we're exclusive.' The Soho House model for talent platforms.
Logan's biotech friend disrupted her industry by going directly to morgues instead of middlemen. 'My middleman is charging me too much money' → go direct to source.
Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution.
Prioritize New York City for high-signal user acquisition via Harvard Club NYC, Cornell Club NYC, AI Circle.
Brand names provide website legitimacy; individual recruiters drive actual success stories. V owns Superposition relationship.
